subtitle: Stiffness and pain at the back of the ankle

strava_risk_triggers.risk_note: Risk rises sharply with consecutive running days and speed work

mild.description: Stiffness in the morning, eases after 10 min of movement
mild.can_run_note: Yes — no speed work, avoid hills
why: Repetitive load causing collagen fibre disruption in the tendon
Tight calves increasing tension on the Achilles insertion
Too much speed work or hill running too soon
relief: Eccentric heel drops on a step — 3 × 15 twice daily
Heel lift insole to reduce tendon load during recovery
Gentle calf massage with a foam roller
prevention: Never stretch a painful Achilles — load it eccentrically instead
Build speed work gradually — no more than one session per week
Avoid sudden increase in hill running
Warm up thoroughly before any fast running

moderate.description: Pain during runs that does not fully ease, tender to squeeze
moderate.can_run_note: Easy flat runs only — stop if pain exceeds 4/10
why: Tendon load exceeding its repair capacity consistently
Reactive tendinopathy — tendon is swollen and irritable
Calf weakness causing tendon to absorb excess force
relief: Isometric calf holds — stand on toes, hold 45s × 5 for pain relief
Heel raise in all footwear temporarily
Avoid barefoot walking and flat shoes
prevention: Eccentric and heavy slow resistance calf programme
Address hip and glute weakness — reduces Achilles load
Monitor training load spikes using the 10% rule

severe.description: Pain at rest, visible swelling, cannot complete a run
severe.can_run_note: Do not run — risk of rupture if ignored
why: Degenerative tendinopathy with significant structural change
Possible partial tear — requires imaging to assess
relief: Complete rest from running for at least 4 weeks
Physiotherapy assessment urgently — especially to rule out tear
Heavy slow resistance programme under physio guidance
PRP injection may be considered for chronic cases
prevention: 12-week structured tendon rehab before returning to running
Never return to speed work until pain-free at easy pace for 4 weeks
Gait analysis to identify loading pattern

see_physio_if: A sudden sharp pain during a run — possible rupture
Visible lump or thickening on the tendon
No improvement after 2 weeks of eccentric loading
